fills: Gambar 4.27 — cross-dataset overall (window=20) AUC matrix: 8-model x 5-dataset (N=500 most-trained cell)
cell_choice_note: N=500 chosen as the most-trained cell (largest training-set size in the sweep) per the brief, for the overall (window=20) matrix.
models_present_note: All 8 models present in every (dataset,N=500,window=20) cell: hcie(ensemble), bkt, dkt, sakt, irt_1pl, greedy_correct_rate, random, static_prior. NOTE: GKT is NOT in this sweep (GKT only appears in the matched-eval cross_dataset_kalman.json tiny runs, pass2).
CRITICAL_FLAG_hcie_is_ensemble: HCIE cells in this sweep are the runtime ENSEMBLE (mastery_after), NOT kalman-alone. Per research_validation/SCALE_SWEEP_SMALL_N.md: the per-cell runs are not in experiment_trajectories and kt_baseline_predictions is empty for them, so there is no per-row kalman posterior to re-score. A kalman-alone F10 would shift HCIE up ~uniformly by the matched-eval delta (~+0.027) but does NOT flip the conclusions. This differs from the canonical seal (single-Kalman m_K).
framing: AUC competitive not superior. Matrix shows HCIE leading on CSEDM, near-floor models (random~0.50, static_prior=0.50) anchoring the bottom, deep models leading on the dense datasets. Per-dataset leadership varies — no universal winner.
